cards.front: Coordination and agreement in group communication
cards.back: Refers to the process of achieving mutual understanding and agreement among group members through effective communication
A coordination mechanism where individuals have exclusive access to a shared resource, preventing simultaneous access and ensuring efficient usage
A coordination mechanism where the actions of one individual directly impact the actions of another individual, creating a dependency between them
The process of assigning and managing resources, such as time, space, and information, within a group to achieve common goals
The shared knowledge and comprehension among group members, essential for effective coordination and agreement in group communication
